title: Distributed Merchant Testing with Controlled Per-Merchant Footprint
narrative: Fraudster uses stolen card credentials across multiple merchants while carefully limiting activity at each individual merchant to avoid merchant-level velocity triggers. Each transaction appears legitimate in isolation—amounts are reasonable, billing information matches, and no single merchant sees unusual volume. However, card-level counters reveal the card is being used across an abnormally high number of distinct merchants in a short time window. The fraudster exploits the fact that most merchant fraud systems only see their own transaction history, not the card's cross-merchant aggregated activity. This pattern is particularly effective against cards with previously low utilization, where the sudden diversity of merchant activity contrasts sharply with historical behavior. The attack maintains per-merchant normalcy while the aggregate card-counter metrics expose the distributed nature of the fraud.
indicators: C8 > 29 (p90), indicating card used across many distinct merchants in rolling window
C14 between 1 and 3, showing limited repeat transactions per merchant
C13 > 24 (p90), elevated count of distinct merchant categories visited
card1_distinct_addr1 > 5, card associated with multiple shipping addresses
C1 > 60 (p90), high overall card transaction counter despite low per-merchant counts
TransactionAmt between 50 and 200, amounts stay within typical cardholder range
C11 < 10, email domain counter remains relatively low suggesting fresh email-card pairings
indicator_explanations: The card has been used at an unusually high number of different merchants recently
Each individual merchant sees only one or two transactions from this card
The card has been used across many different merchant category codes
Multiple different shipping addresses have been associated with this card number
The card's total transaction count is very high compared to cardholder history
Purchase amounts appear normal and consistent with typical spending patterns
The email address hasn't been extensively used with this card across merchants
summary: Card is being used across many different merchants with controlled activity at each location, keeping per-merchant velocity low while card-level counters show abnormal diversity. Individual transactions look normal but aggregated cross-merchant patterns reveal distributed credential abuse.
reasoning: Legitimate cardholders typically concentrate spending at a smaller set of merchants, especially over short time windows. A card appearing at many different merchants with minimal repeat business at each location suggests systematic testing of stolen credentials across multiple fraud targets while deliberately staying under each merchant's individual fraud thresholds.
